extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won the 40.7km time trial by 19 seconds over POGAČAR, while VINGEGAARD secured overall victory with a conservative ride after building an insurmountable lead.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT deliberately soft-pedaled final kilometers after checkpoint dominance to gift stage win to team leader VINGEGAARD, illustrating tactical subordination.
VINGEGAARD rode defensively after dangerous corner wobble, prioritizing safety over stage win once calculating time gap ensured overall victory.
THOMAS secured podium position with second-place TT performance, demonstrating TT specialists peak late in Grand Tours when pure climbers fade.
LAMPAERT emotional despite finishing outside podium, suggesting early tour leaders use opening TT yellow jersey as career-defining motivation beyond stage results.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: dominated time checks before easing off, third stage win matching 2021 pattern of peaking in final TT
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: grass excursion on dangerous descent triggered conservative finish, increased GC lead by 8s despite not targeting stage win
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: lost 27 seconds to VAN AERT despite maximum effort, unable to reduce GC deficit through superior TT skill
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: second place finish shows 2018 winner maintains elite TT form in third week when younger climbers deteriorate
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: reported course reconnaissance to THOMAS pre-ride, revealing Ineos tactical information-sharing protocol for final-week podium defense
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: neutral: emotional at finish despite stage one yellow jersey being primary tour achievement, revealing psychological impact of early leadership
